description: Layered Depot prebaked base images, named by TOOLCHAIN — ELF/Mach-O/PE/wasm are build OUTPUTS, not bases. Each layer adds one toolchain family; a builder FROMs the thinnest base that has what it needs. OPERATING MODEL: each base is built from a VENDORED upstream recipe (untracked-by-default — see docs untracked-by-default), size-optimized (slim variant, multi-stage, stripped), then prebaked (built + pushed to the org registry) and optimized for pull (layer cache / eStargz). We do NOT FROM third-party published images; we replicate + control them. node comes from a vendored node-caged recipe (V8 pointer compression = lower memory) pinned in external-tools.json, NOT apt. base carries sfw (Socket Firewall) + shims so EVERY downstream registry install (npm/pip/cargo) is firewall-guarded. node/pnpm are NOT in the root base — a pure C/C++ toolchain doesn't need them; only .mts-driven builds do, and they COPY --from=node-base. Dockerfiles live in docker/fleet-bases/ (wheelhouse-owned, built centrally by scripts/repo/build-prebakes.mts, NOT cascaded). Build universe = socket-btm + ultrathink; socket-addon/socket-bin/stuie download+verify+republish (build nothing). Single-consumer combos add the extra tool in the leaf Dockerfile, not a shared base: boringssl=c-base+Go+perl, opentui=base+zig, acorn-rust(wasm)=rust-base+wasm32 target.

prebakes.name: base
prebakes.purpose: Language/runtime-agnostic root. OS + git + curl + ccache + sfw + shims — NO node, NO pnpm (a pure C/C++ toolchain doesn't need them). sfw + shims live here so any registry install in ANY downstream layer (pip/cargo/npm) is firewall-guarded; a C-only build that never fetches from a registry simply doesn't exercise it.

prebakes.name: node-base
prebakes.vendorSource: platformatic/node-caged (vendor docker/slim recipe untracked; V8 pointer-compression = lower memory; build our own, do not pull the DockerHub image)
prebakes.purpose: The JS runtime layer: a size-optimized node (from the vendored node-caged pointer-compression recipe, pinned in external-tools.json) + pnpm. Doubles as the JS-CI base the 5 JS repos + wheelhouse pull (lint/test). Because node is needed wherever .mts build scripts run, compiler-base builds that orchestrate via .mts COPY the node binary from here instead of inheriting it — keeping the toolchain bases node-free.

prebakes.name: c-base
prebakes.purpose: C/C++ toolchain for the small native parts (launcher stub, curl, lief, libpq, the bin* utils, dawn, proteus). NODE-FREE — node/pnpm are not inherited; .mts-driven builds COPY --from=node-base. No Go, no wasm (that is what we cut from today's monolith). sfw (from base) guards it IF a build pulls python/rust/etc. musl variant feeds the static SEA pipeline. boringssl lands here but adds Go + perl in its own leaf layer (only C+Go combo).

prebakes.name: node-smol-base
prebakes.purpose: The heavy Node.js/V8 compile, on top of c-base. Split into its own layer for CACHE LOCALITY: the toolchain rarely changes, the Node source changes every version bump. The .mts driver's node comes via COPY --from=node-base, not a baked runtime.

prebakes.name: rust-base
prebakes.purpose: Rust/cargo only — the small/fast no-wasm Rust base. No cmake/clang, no node. sfw (from base) guards crates.io fetches. NAPI links via the bundled C linker; the napi build COPYs node from node-base. acorn-rust is the ONLY rust->wasm consumer, so it adds `rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown` + wasm-bindgen in its own build — same treatment as boringssl(+Go) and opentui(+zig); there is no distinct rust 'wasm SDK' to name a base after.

prebakes.name: go-base
prebakes.purpose: Go toolchain (+ a C compiler for cgo / napi-go). sfw (from base) guards the module proxy. Handles Go->wasm NATIVELY (GOOS=js,GOARCH=wasm — no emscripten), so there is no separate go-wasm base. napi-go builds COPY node from node-base.

prebakes.name: emsdk-base
prebakes.vendorSource: emscripten/emsdk (vendor the pinned SDK install untracked; build + size-optimize our own, do not pull emscripten's published image)
prebakes.purpose: C/C++ -> wasm via Emscripten, on top of c-base. Folds the standalone ubuntu:22.04 wasm Dockerfiles (onnxruntime/yoga today share nothing with the base) onto the shared C layer.

prebakes.name: acorn-base
consumers: ultrathink:acorn cross-language parity job (rust + c++ + go + ts in one run)
prebakes.purpose: Polyglot kitchen-sink for ultrathink/acorn's cross-language parity (blake3) job that runs all 4 backends together. Top of the DAG: emsdk-base already carries c++/cmake/emscripten; this adds rust + go, and COPYs node from node-base. Per-language CI uses the thin single-lang bases; only the all-langs parity job needs this.
